title: Neon Cathedral, Vol. 1
tonic_progression: Em (Aeolian, the dark abandoned nave — the listener enters the ruined cathedral, neon just beginning to hum awake) -> G (Lydian, the first lift — power surges and the laser-lit stained glass begins to glow) -> Bm (Dorian, the flooded marble nave, holographic saints flickering above the still water) -> D (major, the great rose window catches the light, a first radiant lift) -> A (Lydian, the high vaulted ceiling lit in full, light flooding down the columns — a reverent radiance) -> F#m (Aeolian, the crypt below the waterline, the cathedral's darker memory) -> C# / Db (major Lydian, THE RADIANT APEX — full light through every stained-glass panel at once, the choral vocoder swells at their widest, the cathedral ablaze with neon and laser light) -> A (major, the afterglow of the apex, light beginning to soften) -> F#m (Dorian, the holographic saints fading, dusk inside the nave) -> Bm (Aeolian, the neon dimming panel by panel, the water going still and dark) -> G (major, the last warm benediction — a single panel still lit, a hymn of farewell) -> Em (Aeolian, the cathedral returns to dark and silence, the last neon flickering out, a quiet amen)
narrative_arc: Twelve tracks carry the listener through a single night inside a vast abandoned gothic cathedral retrofitted with humming neon and laser-lit stained glass — a slow reverent procession from dark ruin into full radiant light and back into silence. This is a DEVOTIONAL synthwave album, hymn-like and reverb-huge: cathedral-organ pads and wordless choral vocoder swells fill the flooded marble nave, and the album EARNS its major-key and Lydian radiance at its reverent peaks — the light through the stained glass made harmony. Tracks 1-2 are the entrance and the awakening: the listener steps into the dark abandoned nave where the neon is only beginning to hum awake (1, E minor, the drumless opener — the cathedral in ruin and silence), and then power surges and the laser-lit stained glass begins to glow for the first time, the album's first lift into light (2, G Lydian). Tracks 3-5 are the slow ascent into radiance: the flooded marble nave revealed with holographic saints flickering above the still black water and the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF emerging fragmentary (3, B minor Dorian); the great rose window catching the light in a first radiant major lift (4, D major); and the high vaulted ceiling lit in full, light flooding down the columns in a reverent Lydian radiance (5, A Lydian, the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL bloomed wide for the first time). Track 6 is the descent into the crypt below the waterline — the cathedral's darker memory, a reverent shadow gathering before the great light (6, F# minor). Track 7 is THE RADIANT APEX — every stained-glass panel lit at once, the choral vocoder swells at their widest, the cathedral ablaze with neon and laser light in full C#/Db Lydian glory, the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF played whole and radiant (7, the album's peak, BPM 106 — radiance with reverence, not speed; this is a hymn at its climax, not a rave). Tracks 8-11 are the long reverent descent: the afterglow of the apex as light begins to soften (8, A major); the holographic saints fading into dusk inside the nave (9, F# minor Dorian); the neon dimming panel by panel as the water goes still and dark (10, B minor); and a last warm benediction with a single panel still lit, a hymn of farewell (11, G major, the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL warm-but-fading). Track 12 is the closer: the cathedral returns to dark and silence, the last neon flickering out over the flooded marble, the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF rung whole one last time and let fade, a quiet amen resolving home to E minor (12, the drumless close — the night ends, the light gone, the great space dark and still again). A listener moving from the first dark bar to the last should feel a complete act of reverence: a descent into ruin, a rising into light, a blaze of radiant glory, and a slow benediction back into the dark.

recurring_motifs.motif: STAINED-GLASS MOTIF (the album's primary leitmotif) — a bright, faceted crystalline figure voiced as a major-add9 arpeggio (root-fifth-ninth-major-third-octave) on a glass-bell + FM-mallet layer run through a slow chorus and an enormous cathedral convolution-reverb tail, so each note RINGS and refracts like laser light scattering through a panel of stained glass. It is the sonic rendering of the laser-lit stained glass coming alive: cold and dark when the windows are unlit, warm and radiant when the light pours through. This is the album's recurring devotional figure, tracking the night's arc of light — fragmentary and dim when the neon is only awakening, whole and radiant at the apex when every panel blazes, and rung whole one last time and let fade as the cathedral goes dark.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 3 introduces the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF FRAGMENTARY AND DIM — just the upper three notes glinting cold and incomplete, the laser-lit glass only beginning to catch the light above the flooded nave (the listener should hear a crystalline figure that is not yet whole, not yet warm). Track 5 plays it WHOLE AND WARMING for the first time as the high vaulted ceiling lights in full — the complete major-add9 figure ringing radiant through the cathedral reverb, the glass now glowing. Track 7 (radiant apex) plays it WHOLE AND BLAZING at its widest and brightest — every panel lit at once, the figure doubled across octaves and drenched in light, the cathedral's full glory made harmony (the answer to track 3's dim fragment: the same figure, now complete and radiant). Track 11 plays it WHOLE BUT WARM-AND-FADING at the last benediction — full but softening, a single panel still lit, the figure ringing one last warm time as the light withdraws. Track 12 (closer) rings it WHOLE ONE LAST TIME AND LETS IT FADE TO SILENCE — the figure rung complete over the dark flooded marble and allowed to decay into the cathedral reverb until nothing remains, the last light of the stained glass going out, a quiet amen.

recurring_motifs.motif: CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL (the album's devotional voice-texture) — a wide, wordless, slowly-blooming choral pad run through a vocoder and a vast cathedral reverb, voiced as open fifths plus a major third spread across four or five octaves, a hymn-like swell with no lyrics and no phonemes — pure sustained sacred TEXTURE, never a lead vocal. It is the album's voice of reverence, the holographic saints' wordless choir filling the great space. Its arc tracks the cathedral's light: hushed and distant when the nave is dark, bloomed wide and radiant at the apex, and warm-but-fading at the benediction — a devotional crescendo-and-release across the album that mirrors the rise and fall of the neon light.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 2 introduces the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL HUSHED AND DISTANT as power first surges and the stained glass begins to glow — a faint wordless choral pad rising at the edges of the mix, the first breath of the saints' voice in the great space (present but reverent and far). Track 5 brings it WARM AND OPENING mid-mix as the high vaulted ceiling lights in full — the swell widening across octaves, the choir filling the nave for the first time, radiant but still gathering. Track 7 (radiant apex) BLOOMS it AT ITS WIDEST AND BRIGHTEST — the wordless choir spread across five octaves and drenched in light at the cathedral's full glory, the devotional voice at its absolute peak (its single most radiant statement). Track 11 returns it WARM-BUT-FADING at the last benediction — the swell softening and withdrawing, the choir's voice growing distant and tender as the light withdraws, a hymn of farewell sung into the emptying nave. Deliberately ABSENT from the drumless dark bookends (track 1, the ruined silence before the choir wakes, and track 12, where only the stained-glass motif rings and fades — the choir has already gone silent).

recurring_motifs.motif: ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE (the album's foundational sacred bass) — a deep, slow, immense cathedral-organ pedal tone, a very low warm-but-vast sine + organ-pipe layer with a long slow swell and an enormous reverb tail, the sonic rendering of the great pipe organ's lowest pedal notes filling a vast stone space. It is what makes the album feel SACRED and CAVERNOUS and HUGE — the weight and slow surge of cathedral air made audible beneath the harmony, heavier and slower-swelling than any ordinary synth bass. It anchors the reverence: the foundation the whole devotional architecture rests on.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 1 introduces the ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE hushed and dark beneath the ruined nave — a deep slow pedal tone breathing in the empty cathedral before the neon wakes, the foundation present in the silence. Track 3 deepens it beneath the flooded marble nave, the immense slow surge of organ air around the still black water. Track 5 grows it warm-and-vast beneath the lit vaulted ceiling, the sacred foundation as the light floods down. Track 7 (radiant apex) takes it to its fullest and most resonant — the great organ pedal at full depth beneath the blazing stained glass, the cathedral's foundation at the height of its glory. Track 9 returns it slow and dim as the holographic saints fade into dusk, the pedal now the hush the great space settles into. Track 11 holds it warm-and-settled beneath the last benediction, the organ's final low breath beneath the fading light. Deliberately ABSENT in feel from the closer's final bars (track 12), where the drone thins to a single low fading pedal-pulse decaying into silence rather than swelling from below — the great organ going quiet as the cathedral returns to dark.

tracks.composer_notes: OPENER. This track establishes the album's sacred palette and its reverent hush: the ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE enters first (the great pipe organ's lowest pedal tone breathing in the empty cathedral), and the whole space is rendered cavernous and dark by an enormous cathedral reverb. This is the album's drumless dark bookend (paired with the drumless closer, track 12) and tied for its slowest at 82 BPM. The mood is ABANDONED and REVERENT, not menacing — a vast ruined sacred space in silence, the neon only beginning, faintly, to hum awake. The CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL is deliberately ABSENT here (the choir has not yet woken; only the organ and the faintest neon stir). Do NOT let MusicGen add drums, a hard kick, or make this menacing — it is reverent, vast, dark, and anticipatory; the light begins next track. Outro leans toward G major (the relative major) — the power surges and the stained glass prepares to glow as the album's first lift into light begins next track.

tracks.composer_notes: FIRST LIFT / FIRST APPEARANCE of the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL. The swell is introduced HUSHED AND DISTANT — a faint wordless choral pad rising at the edges of the mix, the first breath of the holographic saints' voice in the great space (present but reverent and far; it blooms wider later). The relative-major lift (E minor -> G major) and the bright Lydian raised-4th (C#) are the album's first turn toward radiance as the neon comes alive. Keep the pulse minimal (soft kick on 1, soft snare on 3) and the choir HUSHED — this is awakening, not arrival; the full light is still building. tracks.composer_notes: BRIDGE INTO PEAK / the vaults light in full. The STAINED-GLASS MOTIF is played WHOLE AND WARMING for the first time — its structural debut as a complete figure after the dim fragment in track 3: the full major-add9 glass-bell figure ringing radiant through the cathedral reverb, the glass now glowing (the answer to track 3's cold incomplete glint). The ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE grows warm-and-vast beneath the lit vaults. The ascending fifth (D -> A) and the lift to Lydian (raised-4th D#) make this the brightest key yet — the radiance climbing toward the apex. STRUCTURAL NOTE for the pipeline: this is the bridge-into-peak — the next track (7's slot is reserved for the apex; track 6 is a reverent shadow first). 270s long to give the lit vaults room. All choir is wordless vocoder texture only. Outro E (V of A) reaches toward C#/Db of track 6... NOTE: track 6 descends to F# minor (the crypt shadow) before the apex — the outro here gently darkens toward that shadow rather than going straight to the blaze, so the apex lands harder for the contrast.

The cathedral returns to dark and silence — the last neon flickering out over the flooded marble, a quiet amen. This is the album's HOME TONIC (E minor) returning for the close, mirroring the opener at the identical 82 BPM and drumless: the album is FRAMED between the dark E-minor nave at the start (the light not yet woken) and the dark E-minor silence at the end (the light gone out) — a complete devotional arch. The STAINED-GLASS MOTIF is rung WHOLE ONE LAST TIME and let decay into the cathedral reverb until nothing remains (its final statement: the last light of the stained glass going out). The CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL is deliberately ABSENT (the choir has already gone silent in the dark bookend — only the organ, the last neon flicker, and the fading stained-glass motif remain). The ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE thins to a single low fading pedal-pulse decaying to silence (the great organ going quiet). CRITICALLY, unlike the opener (which leaned OUT toward the relative major as the light began), the closer resolves IN onto a CLOSED, peaceful, RESOLVED E-minor home cadence — the night ended, complete, at peace; NOT an open question. Do NOT let MusicGen add drums, a hard kick, brighten this, or make it menacing — it is reverent, vast, dark, peaceful, and RESOLVED; the quiet amen IS the composition. 270s long (matching the opener) to give the return to dark and silence its full reverent weight.

album_notes.overall_form: ABA reverent arch: Dark Nave -> Awakening -> Ascent into Radiance -> Reverent Shadow -> RADIANT APEX -> Reverent Descent -> Return to Dark over 12 tracks, a symmetric DESCEND-INTO-RUIN, RISE-INTO-LIGHT, BLAZE, then BENEDICT-BACK-INTO-DARK arc — a single night inside a vast abandoned gothic cathedral retrofitted with humming neon and laser-lit stained glass, rendered as a hymn-like, reverb-huge devotional synthwave act. The shape is a true arch capped by a radiant major-key/Lydian apex and bookended by two drumless dark E-minor tracks (the cathedral in silence before the light wakes, and after it goes out). Movements: DARK NAVE & AWAKENING (tracks 1-2): the lone listener enters the dark abandoned nave where the neon only faintly hums awake (1, E minor, drumless, slowest/darkest, the ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE established), and power surges as the laser-lit stained glass begins to glow for the first time, the album's first lift into light (2, G Lydian, the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL introduced hushed-and-distant). ASCENT INTO RADIANCE (tracks 3-5): the flooded marble nave revealed with holographic saints flickering above still black water and the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF emerging fragmentary-and-dim (3, B minor Dorian, first full kit), the great rose window catching the light in a first radiant major lift (4, D major), and the high vaulted ceiling lit in full as the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF rings whole-and-warming and the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL opens warm (5, A Lydian, soaring). REVERENT SHADOW (track 6, F# minor): the descent into the flooded crypt below the waterline, the cathedral's darker memory, the deepest shadow gathering before the greatest light. RADIANT APEX (track 7, C#/Db Lydian): every stained-glass panel lit at once, the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF whole-and-blazing, the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL bloomed at its widest, the ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE fullest — the cathedral ablaze with neon and laser light at the height of its glory, the album's peak BPM (106) and brightest key, radiance with reverence not speed. REVERENT DESCENT (tracks 8-11): the warm afterglow lingering in the aisles (8, A major), the holographic saints fading into dusk (9, F# minor Dorian), the neon dimming panel by panel as the water goes still and dark (10, B minor interlude, the shortest), and a last warm benediction with a single panel still lit (11, G major, the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL and STAINED-GLASS MOTIF warm-and-fading). RETURN TO DARK (track 12, E minor): the cathedral returns to dark and silence, the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF rung whole one last time and let fade, resolving home onto a closed peaceful E-minor cadence — a quiet amen. The dark E-minor bookends (tracks 1 and 12, both drumless, both at 82 BPM) frame the radiant apex at the arch's center; the album is a complete act of reverence from dark to light to dark.
album_notes.key_strategy: A symmetric ASCENT-THROUGH-BRIGHTENING-KEYS / DESCENT-BACK arch that EARNS its major-key and Lydian radiance at the reverent peaks (the light through the stained glass made harmony) and frames the whole with the home minor (E minor) at both dark bookends. The album OPENS dark in E minor (Aeolian, the abandoned nave), lifts to its relative major G (Lydian — the first glow, raised-4th brightness), settles into a luminous B minor (Dorian — the flooded nave), then climbs through ever-brighter MAJORS toward the apex: D major (the rose window, first full major radiance) -> A major (Lydian — the lit vaults, brighter still) — an ASCENDING-FIFTH brightening engine (D up a 5th to A) that is the rising-into-light counterpart of a descending sink. Before the apex, the album steps DOWN into F# minor (Aeolian — the crypt shadow, the relative minor of A), a deliberate deepest-shadow-before-greatest-light contrast, then resolves UP a perfect fifth from that shadow into the RADIANT APEX C#/Db major (Lydian — the brightest, most open key of the album, every panel ablaze; this minor-to-major fifth-lift is the album's central devotional payoff). The descent then mirrors the ascent symmetrically back toward the dark: A major (afterglow) -> F# minor Dorian (the saints fade, the same pitch as the crypt-shadow re-cast luminous-cool) -> B minor (the dimming nave, the same pitch as track 3's luminous nave re-cast Aeolian-dim) -> G major (the last benediction, the SAME key as track 2's awakening lift re-cast warm-and-fading: the first light and the last light share a key) -> E minor (home, the cathedral returns to dark). MODE is the album's light-control: Lydian (raised-4th) for the brightest reverent radiance (tracks 2, 5, 7), plain major for warm radiance (4, 8, 11), Dorian (raised-6th) for luminous-but-reverent minors (3, 9), and natural Aeolian for the dark/dimming/solemn moments (1, 6, 10, 12). The arch's pitch-palette deliberately RHYMES across its halves (G major opens and closes the lit sections; B minor is the nave lit and the nave dimming; F# minor is the crypt-shadow and the fading saints; E minor frames both dark ends) so the descent feels like the same cathedral seen again as the light goes out. The closer resolves onto a CLOSED, peaceful E-minor home cadence (a quiet amen, a complete act — NOT an open question), framing the album as a self-contained devotional arch from dark to light to dark. This is a standalone Vol. 1 — no saga, no cross-album handoff; it opens and closes cleanly.
album_notes.tempo_strategy: A meditative, hymn-paced symmetric arch (band 82-106) — deliberately slow and reverent throughout, because this is devotional music for late-night programmers and 2am study, never frantic: the apex's power is in LIGHT, WIDTH, and REVERB, not velocity. A patient small-incremented rise to a still-restrained radiant apex, then a symmetric gentle wind-down back into the dark. DARK NAVE & AWAKENING 82 (drumless, the dark abandoned nave, the album's slowest, tied with the closer) -> 88 (the first slow pulse as power surges and the glass begins to glow). ASCENT INTO RADIANCE 92 -> 96 -> 100 (the rose window and the lit vaults, the reaches toward the apex). REVERENT SHADOW 104 (the crypt shadow, tempo still climbing even as the harmony darkens, building anticipation). RADIANT APEX 106 (track 7, the BPM ceiling — the cathedral ablaze; even at full radiant glory the album stays hymn-paced at 106, well within the meditative band and far below any racing tempo, because the glory is in light not speed). REVERENT DESCENT 100 -> 94 -> 90 -> 86 (a symmetric gentle wind-down through the back half mirroring the ascent's steps, the interlude at track 10 dipping to 90 for the quietest ambient dimming). RETURN TO DARK 82 (the closer, the album's floor, the SAME tempo as the opener — the two drumless dark bookends framing the album at the identical hymn-slow 82). The band 82-106 is meditative-devotional throughout; the +/- increments per track are small and patient (mostly 4-6 BPM), creating a slow reverent forward-then-back motion without ever breaking the hymn-like flow or rising into anything frantic. All BPMs within the 80-118 validation range (album range 82-106).
album_notes.motif_strategy: Three album-local motifs (no saga recurrence — this is a standalone Vol. 1; all three are saga_recurring: false), each tracking the night's arc of light. (1) STAINED-GLASS MOTIF (primary leitmotif) — a bright faceted major-add9 arpeggio on a glass-bell + FM-mallet layer through slow chorus and an enormous cathedral reverb, the sonic rendering of the laser-lit stained glass coming alive (cold/dark when unlit, warm/radiant when the light pours through). Its arc IS the night's light: introduced FRAGMENTARY-AND-DIM (just the upper three notes, cold and incomplete) as the glass first catches light above the flooded nave (track 3); played WHOLE-AND-WARMING for the first time as the vaults light in full (track 5); WHOLE-AND-BLAZING at its widest and brightest, doubled across octaves and drenched in light, at the radiant apex when every panel is lit (track 7); WHOLE-BUT-WARM-AND-FADING at the last benediction, full but softening, a single panel still lit (track 11); and rung WHOLE-ONE-LAST-TIME-AND-LET-FADE over the dark flooded marble, decaying into reverb until nothing remains, at the close (track 12). (2) CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL (devotional voice-texture) — a wide wordless slowly-blooming choral pad through a vocoder and vast reverb, open fifths + major third across four-five octaves, pure sustained sacred TEXTURE with no lyrics or phonemes, never a lead vocal (the holographic saints' wordless choir). Its arc is a devotional crescendo-and-release mirroring the neon's rise and fall: HUSHED-AND-DISTANT as power first surges (track 2); WARM-AND-OPENING mid-mix as the vaults light (track 5); BLOOMED at its widest and brightest across five octaves at the apex (track 7, its single most radiant statement); and WARM-BUT-FADING, the choir's voice growing distant, at the last benediction (track 11). Deliberately ABSENT from the drumless dark bookends (tracks 1 and 12 — the choir not yet woken / already gone silent). (3) ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE (foundational sacred bass) — a deep slow immense cathedral-organ pedal tone (low sine + organ-pipe layer, long slow swell, enormous reverb tail), the great pipe organ's lowest pedal notes filling a vast stone space, what makes the album feel SACRED and CAVERNOUS and HUGE. Introduced hushed-and-dark beneath the ruined nave (track 1, establishing the foundation in the silence); deepened beneath the flooded marble nave (3); grown warm-and-vast beneath the lit vaults (5); fullest-and-most-resonant beneath the blazing stained glass at the apex (7); slow-and-dim as the saints fade (9); warm-and-settled beneath the last benediction (11); and thinned to a single low fading pedal-pulse decaying to silence at the close (12, the great organ going quiet). The three motifs are layered so the cathedral feels alive: the ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE is the sacred foundation present from the first dark bar, the CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL is the reverent voice that wakes and blooms and fades with the light, and the STAINED-GLASS MOTIF is the light itself made melodic — dim, then radiant, then gone.
album_notes.tracklist_continuity: Each track ends on a pitch that primes the next track's tonic, the harmonic glue of the reverent arch — a listener won't consciously track the relationships, but the procession from dark to light to dark will feel inevitable rather than abrupt. Specifically: 1 ends leaning toward G major (the relative major of E minor — the dark nave begins to glow). 2 ends on D (V of G, rerooted toward track 3's B minor region — the flooded nave revealed). 3 ends on F# (V of B minor, rerooted toward track 4's D major — the rose window catches the light). 4 ends on A (V of D, rerooted as track 5's A major — the lit vaults). 5 ends on E (V of A) but the outro gently DARKENS toward track 6's F# minor (the crypt shadow before the apex, so the apex lands harder for the contrast). 6 ends on C# (V of F# minor, rerooted as the radiant apex tonic of track 7's C#/Db major — the deepest shadow resolving up into the greatest light, the album's central devotional payoff). 7 ends softening on G#/Ab (V of A, rerooted as track 8's A major — the afterglow begins). 8 ends on E (V of A, rerooted toward track 9's F# minor — the saints fade). 9 ends on C# (V of F# minor, rerooted toward track 10's B minor — the neon dims). 10 ends on F# (V of B minor, rerooted toward track 11's G major — the last benediction). 11 ends on D (V of G, rerooted toward track 12's E minor — the home tonic returning, the cathedral going dark). 12 resolves home onto a CLOSED, peaceful E-minor home cadence — a quiet amen, the album complete (NOT an open question; this is a standalone Vol. 1 that opens and closes cleanly). The arch's continuity deliberately RHYMES: the E-minor home frames both dark bookends, G major glues the first and last lit moments, and the ascent's keys reappear on the descent re-cast (B minor lit then dimming, F# minor crypt-shadow then fading-saints) so the descent feels like the same cathedral seen again as the light withdraws. The deepest-shadow-before-greatest-light pivot (F# minor crypt -> C#/Db Lydian apex, a minor-to-major fifth-lift at the arch's center) is the single most important transition: it must make the apex's blaze land as a resurrection of light after the crypt's solemn dark.
album_notes.execution_notes_for_pipeline: MusicGen / ACE-Step is the renderer of record (license risk accepted). Every musicgen_prompt is explicit about 'instrumental' and 'no vocals'; ALL choir/vocoder references are phrased as 'wordless choral vocoder swell' or 'wordless choir pad' (textural, no lyrics, no phonemes, never a lead vocal) — do NOT relax this in any per-track override (the cathedral's choir and vocoder are PAD/TEXTURE only; the album is fully instrumental). Per-track durations sum to 2940 seconds (49.0 minutes) for the 12 — comfortably over the 45-minute album floor. Long tracks: 1 (270s, the dark nave needs room to establish the sacred space), 5 (270s, the lit vaults), 7 (300s, the radiant apex, longest — the full blaze needs room to breathe), 12 (270s, the return to dark and silence). Interlude track 10 is the shortest at 210s (the quiet dimming). The rest sit at 240s. All BPMs within the 80-118 validation range (album range 82-106). COVER ASSETS: per the future-albums native-aspect-gen rule, generate covers at native SDXL aspect buckets (1024 square + 1344x768 + 768x1344) from scratch rather than outpainting; every cover_prompt ends with 'no text, 1024x1024' and keeps faces hidden (lone-white-robed-figure-from-behind, holographic saints faceless/translucent) to avoid realistic-face generation. The 12 covers share one neon-cathedral visual family (deep indigo/blue-violet/near-black lit by magenta/cyan/gold neon and laser-lit stained glass over flooded black-marble water; the lone white-robed figure recurs from behind) and narrate the dark->awaken->ascend->blaze->dim->dark arc; track 7 is the HERO cover (the full blaze). WEATHER ROUTING — the per-album weather overlay (TWC panel) is mandatory on all videos per the 'we always want weather' rule. This is a standalone non-saga, non-geographic THEME (an abstract/fictional cathedral, not a real place), so there is no natural geographic anchor; route it to the default US 4-city set as an explicit by-exception choice (matching the Vol. 4 precedent where default US cities shipped by exception) — OR, if a thematically reverent set is preferred, pick 4 US cities famous for grand cathedrals (recommend New York / Washington DC / Chicago / San Francisco — all have major neo-gothic cathedrals and full NWS coverage). Either way confirm `nightdrive-encoder::weather::region_for` has no 'cathedral'/'neon' keyword branch (it will fall through to a hashed US region as-shipped, which is acceptable for this non-geographic theme; a deliberate US-cathedral-cities override is the nicer option — flag for human choice at encode time). PUBLISH STRATEGY: per the sync-drop rule for future albums, target a 1-shot synchronized public flip at one anchor timestamp (run-album --publish-at), NOT per-track trickle. Mind the YouTube caps for a 12-track album: the ~10/day channel rolling-24h cap, the 6/day GCP per-project video.insert cap, and Pacific-midnight resets — span the upload across a reset or stagger (publish-staggered 6/day) and set the sync-drop anchor accordingly, per the Vol. 2/Vol. 3 lessons. DANGER ZONE: danger_zone_keys is [] (none flagged). Per the album_title_danger_zone double-hit rule, all track titles were checked to be DESCRIPTIVE of the neon-cathedral scene and NOT bare famous Latin-liturgy titles (deliberately AVOIDED 'Requiem', 'Ave Maria', 'Dies Irae', 'Agnus Dei', 'Sanctus', 'Kyrie' as titles — those collide with famous sacred/choral works and film scores) and NOT named tracks from famous gothic/sacred film scores: 'The Dark Nave', 'Power Surge', 'Flooded Marble', 'The Rose Window', 'Vaults of Light', 'Beneath the Waterline', 'Every Panel Ablaze', 'Afterglow in the Aisles', 'The Saints Fade', 'Dimming the Panels', 'Last Benediction', 'Return to Dark and Silence' — all original scene-descriptive titles, none matching a known sacred/choral/gothic-film soundtrack track title.
album_notes.differentiation_from_prior_albums: Neon Cathedral is the catalog's DEVOTIONAL / SACRED-SPACE album and its first true HYMN-LIKE, reverb-huge work — a standalone Vol. 1 (no saga, no cross-album handoff). Where the catalog's prior arcs lived mostly in minor/modal darkness (Neo-Tokyo's descending Phrygian, Tron's all-minor ring-form) or in the Lost Worlds saga's specific narrative throughlines, Neon Cathedral is built to EARN MAJOR-KEY AND LYDIAN RADIANCE at its reverent peaks: it is the catalog's brightest album at its apex (C#/Db Lydian, every panel ablaze), but it earns that light through a true symmetric arch — dark E-minor ruin -> ascent through ever-brighter majors (G Lydian, D major, A Lydian) -> a deliberate crypt-shadow (F# minor) -> the radiant apex -> a symmetric reverent descent back into dark E-minor silence. Its sonic identity is distinct: CATHEDRAL-ORGAN PADS (not the analog/DX7 pad palettes of the drive/city albums), WORDLESS CHORAL-VOCODER SWELLS (a sacred voice-texture, never used as the catalog's lead-melody focus), and an ENORMOUS cathedral CONVOLUTION REVERB on everything (the album's signature — the vast stone space made audible), anchored by an ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE foundation. Tempo-wise it shares the catalog's meditative-low philosophy (band 82-106, hymn-paced throughout, the apex's power in light/width/reverb not speed) — but it is the most REVERENT and SPACIOUS album, the closest the catalog comes to sacred minimalism / ambient devotion. Its three motifs (STAINED-GLASS, CHORAL-VOCODER SWELL, ORGAN-PEDAL DRONE) are album-local and built around the single conceit of a vast cathedral coming alive with neon light and going dark again — the light itself made melodic. Reference vibe anchors: Brian Eno's sacred-ambient vastness + Mike Oldfield's cathedral-scale long-form + Vangelis's hymn-like grandeur + Hans Zimmer/Olafur Arnalds organ-and-choir reverence, crossed with FM-84/The Midnight's melodic warmth and the locked NightDrive synthwave aesthetic — a flooded neon cathedral as a single devotional act, dark to light to dark, for late-night coders who want reverence rather than drive. NO prior album is hymn-like, organ-and-choir-built, reverb-as-signature, or EARNS Lydian/major radiance through a sacred-space arch; Neon Cathedral opens a new devotional lane in the catalog.
